response.justification: The candidate is a strong, versatile engineer with deep web and full-stack experience, but the role demands a hands-on C++ specialist for industrial automation. His only professional C++ work was a roughly seven-month stint at National Instruments in 2014-2015, and C++ does not even appear in his current skills list. He has no exposure to MQTT, industrial protocols, IEC 62443, or Secure Development Lifecycle practices. His DLang low-latency systems work at Dunnhumby shows real high-performance engineering ability, but the core skill match is too weak for this position.
strengths: High-performance, low-latency systems experience (async I/O, fibers, caching) at scale in the DLang work at Dunnhumby
Broad polyglot engineering background with strong testing culture (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development)
Proven mentoring and knowledge-sharing track record through workshops, courses, and open-source projects
concerns: Minimal professional C++ experience — about seven months, ending in 2015, with no C++ listed in current skills
No industrial automation, IoT/MQTT, message-broker, or IEC 62443 / SDL security background
Career trajectory is centered on web/Node.js/TypeScript product work, far from embedded/industrial systems

key_factors.factor: Industrial automation and security standards exposure
